steps.title: Identify the slope
steps.content: $F$ is linear in $x$ with slope $\tfrac{9}{5}$, so each $1$-kelvin increase raises $F$ by $\tfrac{9}{5}$ degrees.

steps.title: Scale by the change
steps.content:
For a 4.2-kelvin increase, $$\Delta F=\tfrac{9}{5}\cdot 4.2=7.56.$$

Only the change in temperature matters here; the intercept of the conversion formula does not affect how much $F$ changes when $x$ increases.
